methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $10.0B total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $1.8B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
OpenStar, a Seed-stage climate and energy company founded by Ratu Mataira that has raised $27M+, is valued against a comparable universe of 9 private and 1 public peer company in the frontier energy and climate sector. The peer set includes later-stage Series A companies such as Watershed ($1.8B), Graphyte ($200M), Fourth Power ($150M), and Steady Energy ($110M).

Two valuation methods were applied. The Stage-Progression approach, using the $27M+ raised as a proxy for a $10.0B implicit asset base and applying a 2.5x multiplier typical of Seed-to-next-stage progression, implies a valuation of $25.0B. The Peer-Median method anchors to the median valuation of four same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables, which stands at $175M, with a range spanning $110M to $1.8B across the sample.

The triangulation of these two methods produces a low estimate of $149M, a median of $12.6B, and a high of $28.7B. The wide dispersion reflects material tension between stage-progression methodology and peer-median benchmarking. Key uncertainties include the appropriateness of the $10.0B base-value proxy given disclosed fundraising, the comparability of Series A peers to a Seed-stage company, and the significant valuation dispersion within the peer set itself—particularly the $1.8B outlier (Watershed). A post-money valuation in the $150M–$500M range appears most defensible pending further operational and market validation.
